message:
fix(signal): close sigaction handler-install TOCTOU window

REVIEW round 2: sigaction installed the kernel-level trampoline via
sys_rt_sigaction BEFORE updating the per-signal handler slot that the
trampoline reads on every delivery. A signal pending at install time
(or arriving in the syscall window) would dispatch through the slot's
PREVIOUS contents — invoking the previous user handler, or being
silently dropped if the slot was still zero (first install for that
signal). Either outcome breaks the user's contract that sigaction
returning success means subsequent deliveries reach the new handler.

Fix: write the new handler+flags into the slot BEFORE rt_sigaction.
On syscall failure, roll the slot back to its prior value so the
trampoline never dispatches a handler the kernel never accepted. The
trampoline addresses are static, so writing the slot before kernel
install is safe — if the kernel hasn't yet installed our trampoline,
signals follow the pre-existing kernel disposition (equivalent to
the user not having called sigaction at all).

Regression test sigaction_pending_signal_invokes_newly_installed_handler
exercises the race deterministically: blocks SIGUSR2, queues a pending
delivery, swaps the handler from A to B while pending, unblocks, and
asserts B (not A) ran.

Full abi suite: 2976/2976 pass. (DB lock contention from earlier
stuck `br sync` processes prevents `br create` for this defect; bug
description is captured here in lieu of a bead.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
